subject: physics
question_text: Which of the following is not the name of a physical quantity?
solution_text: Kilogram is a unit of measurement (for mass), not a physical quantity itself.

subject: physics
question_text: The standard formatting used to represent the magnitude of a physical quantity is given by (where u = unit, n = numerical value):
solution_text: The magnitude of a physical quantity remains constant, so nu = constant, which implies n ∝ 1/u.

subject: physics
question_text: Light year is a unit of:
solution_text: A light year is the distance traveled by light in a vacuum in one year.

subject: physics
question_text: Which one of the following has the dimensions of pressure?

subject: physics
question_text: The dimensions of RC, where R is resistance and C is capacitance, are same as that of:
solution_text: The product of resistance (R) and capacitance (C) gives the time constant (τ), which has the dimensions of time [T].

subject: physics
question_text: The fundamental gravitational constant G has the dimensional formula:
solution_text: From Newton's law of gravitation, F = G(m₁m₂)/r². Rearranging gives G = Fr² / (m₁m₂). Substituting dimensions: [MLT⁻²][L²] / [M²] = [M⁻¹ L³ T⁻²].

subject: physics
question_text: The dimensional formula of magnetic flux is:
solution_text: Magnetic flux Φ = B · A. Since Force F = I · l · B ⇒ B = F / (I · l). Thus, Φ = (F · A) / (I · l) = [MLT⁻²][L²] / ([A][L]) = [M¹L²T⁻²A⁻¹].

subject: physics
question_text: Which of the following pairs have the same dimensional formula?
solution_text: Both torque (Force × distance) and work (Force × displacement) share the same dimensional formula: [M L² T⁻²].
